pattern: Walk clockwise in a repeating East-South-West-North pattern where each direction pair shares an increasing step length, recording only cells that fall inside the grid boundary.
hints: The spiral alternates through four directions; each pair of consecutive turns shares the same run length before that length grows by one.
You can walk the full spiral path — even through out-of-bounds positions — but only append a coordinate to the result when it lies inside the grid.
Repeat direction changes in the cycle E→S→W→N, increment the step length after every two turns, and stop once you have collected all rows×cols grid cells.

edge_inputs.description: single-row grid starting at the left edge, spiral is just a rightward sweep
5x6 grid starting near the middle, exercises many out-of-bounds passes
1x1 grid, only one cell to visit
single column grid, spiral collapses to vertical traversal
start at bottom-right corner, spiral exits and re-enters many times
start at top-left corner of a wide grid
1x100 row, start at right end, spiral is leftward

stress.description: random grid dimensions and random valid starting positions
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    rows = rng.randint(1, min(100, size))
    cols = rng.randint(1, min(100, size))
    rStart = rng.randint(0, rows - 1)
    cStart = rng.randint(0, cols - 1)
    return [rows, cols, rStart, cStart]
stress.note: covers varied grid shapes and starting positions including off-center and corner starts
